medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with persistent erythema/swelling of LLE since last month. Symptoms started 4 weeks ago with cellulitis, treated with Keflex and Bactrim. Pain started as a small spot above the medial malleolus 2 weeks ago. Followed by symptoms worsened and reviewed with PCP and an ultrasound performed which revealed no drainable collection. Presented again to the ED with persistent cellulitis and was treated with IV vancomycin. Condition improved. Swelling has now spread to dorsum of left foot. No drainage, pruritus, or other acute symptoms noted. History of a previous left lower extremity DVT treated with Coumadin.
medical_history.past_medical_history: - Obesity (BMI 41) - Hysterectomy for fibroid uterus and significant anemia - Complete DVT treated with Coumadin and transitioned to baby ASA with prior hypercoagulable workup negative.
medical_history.family_history: Mother: alive with schizophrenia, seizures, ETOH abuse, HTN, and recent cellulitis. No known blood disorders or autoimmune diseases in family.

physical_examination.general_examination: VS T 98.4, BP 131/85, HR 66, RR 18, 100% RA. Patient is alert and oriented, obese and in no acute distress.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: LLE swollen dorsum of foot and calf, warm, reddish, hard. Right lower extremity cooler, no swelling. Neurological examination normal.
